content.text: The fader is a multi-purpose control that can be assigned to different parameters per group. Fader movements can be recorded into the sequencer for automation playback.

content.caption: Fader slider and FADER mode button

content.text: By default, the fader controls group LEVEL (volume). To assign a different parameter, hold the FADER button and press a number key:

content.title: Per-Group Assignment
content.text: Each group can have a different fader assignment. This lets you control volume on one group, filter on another, and pitch on a third.
Simply move the fader up or down to adjust the assigned parameter for the currently selected group. The effect is heard immediately on all sounds in that group.
Fader movements can be recorded into the sequencer. Enable RECORD, start playback, and move the fader. Your movements are captured and will play back with the pattern.

items: Press PLAY to start the sequencer
Move the fader while the pattern plays
The fader automation plays back with the pattern

content.text: To erase fader automation, hold ERASE while moving the fader during playback. This clears recorded fader movements from the current group's pattern.

content.title: Automation Ideas
content.text: Try automating: filter sweeps for build-ups, volume for ducking, pitch for risers, or time stretch for trippy effects.
To reset the fader to its default position (typically center or maximum), press SHIFT + FADER. This is useful when you want to return to a neutral state.

items: Filter automation: Assign LPF and sweep for classic house/techno builds
Performance volume: Use LEVEL for live mixing between groups
Pitch effects: Assign PITCH for DJ-style pitch bends
Time warping: TIME stretch for experimental tempo changes
